{"problem_standardized_math":"Suppose that, according to equation (3.58), a large value of $\\phi(\\alpha_0)$ can lead to a very small next step length $\\alpha_1$. Give an example of a function and a step length $\\alpha_0$ for which this occurs. (In practice, drastic changes in the step-length estimate are undesirable, so one typically imposes a lower bound, for example $\\rho = 0.1$, and defines the new step length by $\\alpha_i = \\max(\\rho\\alpha_{i-1}, \\hat{\\alpha}_i)$, where $\\hat{\\alpha}_i$ is the minimizer of the interpolant.)"}
